title: OpenAPI
description: Root object of an OpenAPI 3.1 document.

__schema0.title: openapi
__schema0.description: OpenAPI version. Must be '3.1.x' for OpenAPI 3.1 documents.

__schema3.title: version
__schema3.description: The version of the API document (not the OpenAPI spec version).

__schema4.title: summary
__schema4.description: A short summary of the API.

__schema5.title: description
examples: A sample API for managing pets
This API handles user authentication and profile management
__schema5.description: A description of the API. CommonMark syntax MAY be used for rich text.

__schema6.title: termsOfService
__schema6.description: A URL to the Terms of Service for the API.

__schema7.title: Contact
__schema7.description: Contact information for the exposed API.

__schema8.title: name
__schema8.description: The identifying name of the contact person/organization.

__schema9.title: url
__schema9.description: The URL pointing to the contact information. Must be a valid URL.

__schema10.title: email
__schema10.description: The email address of the contact person/organization.

__schema12.title: License
__schema12.description: License information for the exposed API.

__schema13.title: License Name
__schema13.description: The license name used for the API.

__schema14.title: License URL
__schema14.description: A URL to the license used for the API.

__schema15.title: License Identifier
__schema15.description: An SPDX license expression for the API.

__schema18.title: jsonSchemaDialect
__schema18.description: The default JSON Schema dialect for Schema Objects.

__schema22.title: url
__schema22.description: A URL to the target host. Supports server variables in {braces}. May be relative.

__schema23.title: description
__schema23.description: An optional description of the server.

__schema24.title: variables
__schema24.description: A map of server variables for URL template substitution.

__schema26.title: ServerVariable
__schema26.description: A server variable for URL template substitution. Use {variableName} in the server URL.

__schema27.title: enum
__schema27.description: An enumeration of valid string values for this variable.

__schema29.title: default
__schema29.description: The default value for substitution if not supplied.

__schema30.title: description
__schema30.description: An optional description for the server variable.

__schema33.title: paths
__schema33.description: Available paths and operations. Optional if webhooks is provided.

__schema34.title: Paths
__schema34.description: Holds the relative paths to the individual endpoints.

__schema36.title: PathItem
__schema36.description: Describes the operations available on a single path.

__schema38.title: Reference
__schema38.description: A reference to another component in the specification.

__schema47.title: Operation
__schema47.description: Describes a single API operation on a path.

__schema48.title: tags
__schema48.description: A list of tags for API documentation grouping.

__schema50.title: summary
__schema50.description: A short summary of the operation. Keep under ~120 characters.

__schema51.title: description
__schema51.description: A verbose description. CommonMark syntax MAY be used.

__schema52.title: operationId
__schema52.description: Unique identifier for the operation. Used for code generation.

__schema53.title: parameters
__schema53.description: Parameters for this operation, combined with path-level parameters.

__schema55.title: Reference Object
__schema55.description: A reference to another component in the specification.

examples.description: A user is a type of person.
A pet is a type of animal.
A pet is a type of animal.

__schema56.title: summary
__schema56.description: A short summary which by default SHOULD override that of the referenced component.

__schema57.title: description
__schema57.description: A description which by default SHOULD override that of the referenced component.

__schema60.title: name
__schema60.description: The name of the parameter. Case-sensitive.

__schema61.title: in
__schema61.description: Location of the parameter. 'path' parameters must have required=true.

__schema62.title: description
__schema62.description: A description of the parameter. CommonMark syntax MAY be used.

__schema67.description: For query parameters only. Allows empty values (?param=).
__schema67.title: allowEmptyValue

__schema69.title: style
__schema69.description: Serialization style. Defaults vary by 'in': query/cookie='form', path/header='simple'.

__schema70.title: explode
__schema70.description: For arrays/objects. When true, each value gets its own parameter.

__schema71.description: For query parameters. When true, allows reserved characters without encoding.
__schema71.title: allowReserved

__schema74.title: Schema Object
__schema74.description: A Schema Object defines the shape of a JSON value.

__schema75.title: Type Array Schema
__schema75.description: OpenAPI 3.1 (JSON Schema) allows type to be an array of primitive types.

__schema76.title: title
__schema76.description: A title for the schema, used for documentation.

__schema77.title: description
__schema77.description: A description of the schema. CommonMark syntax MAY be used.

__schema78.title: default
__schema78.description: The default value for this schema.

__schema79.title: example
__schema79.description: An example value for this schema.

__schema82.title: enum
__schema82.description: An array of valid values for this schema.

__schema84.title: const
__schema84.description: A constant value that this schema must match.

__schema86.title: Discriminator
__schema86.description: When request bodies or response payloads may be one of a number of different schemas, a discriminator object can be used to aid in serialization, deserialization, and validation.

__schema87.title: propertyName
__schema87.description: The name of the property in the payload that will hold the discriminator value.

__schema88.title: mapping
__schema88.description: An object to hold mappings between payload values and schema names or references.

__schema93.title: XML
__schema93.description: A metadata object that allows for more fine-tuned XML model definitions.

__schema94.title: name
__schema94.description: Replaces the name of the element/attribute used for the described schema property.

__schema95.title: namespace
__schema95.description: The URI of the namespace definition.

__schema96.title: prefix
__schema96.description: The prefix to be used for the name.

__schema97.description: Declares whether the property definition translates to an attribute instead of an element.
__schema97.title: attribute

__schema99.description: May be used only for an array definition. Signifies whether the array is wrapped or not.
__schema99.title: wrapped

__schema103.title: ExternalDocumentation
__schema103.description: Allows referencing an external resource for extended documentation.

__schema104.title: description
__schema104.description: A short description of the target documentation.

__schema105.title: url
__schema105.description: The URL for the target documentation.

__schema107.title: readOnly
__schema107.description: When true, the property is only returned in responses, not accepted in requests.

__schema108.title: writeOnly
__schema108.description: When true, the property is only accepted in requests, not returned in responses.

__schema109.title: deprecated
__schema109.description: When true, indicates this schema is deprecated and should be avoided.

__schema113.title: String Object
__schema113.description: A String Object defines the shape of a string value.

__schema115.title: format
__schema115.description: Semantic format hint for string values. Common formats: date-time, email, uuid, uri, hostname, ipv4, byte, binary.

__schema116.title: Pattern
__schema116.description: A regular expression pattern the string must match.

__schema121.title: Number Object
__schema121.description: A Number Object defines the shape of a number value.

__schema123.title: format
__schema123.description: Number precision format. Use 'float' for 32-bit single-precision or 'double' (default) for 64-bit double-precision IEEE 754.

__schema124.title: multipleOf
__schema124.description: Require the number to be a multiple of this value.

__schema126.title: minimum
__schema126.description: Inclusive lower bound for the number.

__schema128.title: maximum
__schema128.description: Inclusive upper bound for the number.

__schema130.title: exclusiveMinimum
__schema130.description: Exclusive lower bound (instance must be > this value).

__schema132.title: exclusiveMaximum
__schema132.description: Exclusive upper bound (instance must be < this value).

__schema134.title: Integer Object
__schema134.description: An Integer Object defines the shape of an integer value.

__schema136.title: format
__schema136.description: Integer size format. Use 'int32' for signed 32-bit (-2B to 2B) or 'int64' for signed 64-bit integers.

__schema137.title: multipleOf
__schema137.description: Require the integer to be a multiple of this value.

__schema139.title: minimum
__schema139.description: Inclusive lower bound for the integer.

__schema141.title: maximum
__schema141.description: Inclusive upper bound for the integer.

__schema143.title: exclusiveMinimum
__schema143.description: Exclusive lower bound (instance must be > this value).

__schema145.title: exclusiveMaximum
__schema145.description: Exclusive upper bound (instance must be < this value).

__schema147.title: Boolean Object
__schema147.description: A Boolean Object defines the shape of a boolean value.

__schema149.title: Null Object
__schema149.description: A Null Object defines the shape of a null value.

__schema151.title: Array Object
__schema151.description: An Array Object defines the shape of an array value.

__schema155.title: prefixItems
__schema155.description: Tuple validation: schemas for items by index (array position).

__schema157.title: contains
__schema157.description: Require the array to contain at least one item matching this subschema.

__schema169.title: Object Object
__schema169.description: An Object Object defines the shape of an object value.

__schema193.title: Composition Schema
__schema193.description: A schema using composition keywords (allOf, anyOf, oneOf, not, if/then/else) without a type constraint.

__schema194.title: allOf
__schema194.description: Require the instance to validate against all subschemas.

__schema196.title: anyOf
__schema196.description: Require the instance to validate against at least one subschema.

__schema198.title: oneOf
__schema198.description: Require the instance to validate against exactly one subschema.

__schema200.title: not
__schema200.description: Require the instance to NOT validate against the given subschema.

__schema202.title: if
__schema202.description: Conditional validation: if the instance matches this schema, apply then; otherwise apply else.

__schema206.title: else
__schema206.description: Subschema applied when if does not match.

__schema209.title: examples
__schema209.description: Multiple named examples. Mutually exclusive with 'example'.

__schema216.title: externalValue
__schema216.description: A URL that points to the literal example.

__schema218.title: content
__schema218.description: For complex parameters. Mutually exclusive with 'schema'.

__schema220.title: MediaType
__schema220.description: Each Media Type Object provides schema and examples for the media type identified by its key.

__schema228.title: Encoding
__schema228.description: A single encoding definition applied to a single schema property.

__schema229.title: contentType
__schema229.description: The Content-Type for encoding a specific property.

__schema232.title: Header
__schema232.description: The Header Object follows the structure of the Parameter Object.

__schema233.title: Header
__schema233.description: The Header Object follows the structure of the Parameter Object.

__schema234.title: description
__schema234.description: A brief description of the parameter.

__schema235.description: Determines whether this parameter is mandatory.
__schema235.title: required

__schema237.description: Specifies that a parameter is deprecated.
__schema237.title: deprecated

__schema239.description: Sets the ability to pass empty-valued parameters.
__schema239.title: allowEmptyValue

__schema241.title: style
__schema241.description: Describes how the parameter value will be serialized.

__schema279.title: Response
__schema279.description: Describes a single response from an API Operation.

__schema280.title: Response
__schema280.description: Describes a single response from an API Operation.

__schema288.title: Link
__schema288.description: The Link object represents a possible design-time link for a response.

__schema289.title: Link
__schema289.description: The Link object represents a possible design-time link for a response.

__schema290.title: operationId
__schema290.description: The name of an existing, resolvable OAS operation, as defined with a unique operationId.

__schema291.title: operationRef
__schema291.description: A relative or absolute URI reference to an OAS operation.

__schema292.title: parameters
__schema292.description: A map representing parameters to pass to an operation as specified with operationId or identified via operationRef.

__schema295.title: requestBody
__schema295.description: A literal value or {expression} to use as a request body when calling the target operation.

__schema304.description: Marks this operation as deprecated. Defaults to false.

__schema305.title: security
__schema305.description: Security requirements for this operation. Overrides root-level security.

__schema306.title: SecurityRequirement
__schema306.description: Lists the required security schemes for this operation.

__schema324.title: webhooks
__schema324.description: Incoming webhooks that the API can receive.

__schema327.title: Components
__schema327.description: Holds a set of reusable objects for different aspects of the OAS.

additionalProperties.title: SecurityScheme
additionalProperties.description: Defines a security scheme that can be used by the operations.

__schema337.title: name
__schema337.description: The name of the header, query, or cookie parameter.

__schema338.title: in
__schema338.description: Location of the API key: 'header' (most common), 'query', or 'cookie'.

__schema339.title: description
__schema339.description: A description of the security scheme.

__schema341.title: HttpSecurityScheme
__schema341.description: HTTP authentication security scheme (Basic, Bearer, etc.).

__schema344.title: bearerFormat
__schema344.description: Hint for bearer token format. Only applicable when scheme='bearer'.

__schema345.title: description
__schema345.description: A description of the security scheme.

__schema354.title: flows
__schema354.description: Allows configuration of the supported OAuth Flows.

__schema356.title: OAuthFlow
__schema356.description: Configuration details for a supported OAuth Flow.

__schema357.title: authorizationUrl
__schema357.description: The authorization URL to be used for this flow.

__schema358.title: tokenUrl
__schema358.description: The token URL to be used for this flow.

__schema359.title: refreshUrl
__schema359.description: The URL to be used for obtaining refresh tokens.

__schema360.title: scopes
__schema360.description: The available scopes for the OAuth2 security scheme.

__schema373.title: openIdConnectUrl
__schema373.description: OpenID Connect URL to discover OAuth2 configuration values.

additionalProperties.title: Callback
additionalProperties.description: A map of possible out-of-band callbacks related to the parent operation.

__schema379.title: Callback
__schema379.description: A map of possible out-of-band callbacks related to the parent operation.

__schema388.title: description
__schema388.description: A description for the tag. CommonMark syntax MAY be used.
